title: Grouping Columns and Collapsing in Main Table
excerpt: My team and I currently use all available tools to keep our columns organized, such as hidden columns, custom views, and filters. However, with a large number of columns in our main table, it still feels cluttered and di…

posts.author: zs2technologies (Erin)
posts.cookedHtml:
My team and I currently use all available tools to keep our columns organized, such as hidden columns, custom views, and filters. However, with a large number of columns in our main table, it still feels cluttered and difficult to navigate.

I’d like to request a feature that allows us to group columns (e.g., “Colours” group > Red, Blue, Green) and collapse or expand the entire group for easier workflow management.

While it is currently possible to collapse individual columns, this isn’t an ideal solution, especially when the column name isn’t visible while collapsed. This requires users to expand each one to identify its contents, which slows down productivity and undermines the benefit of collapsing in the first place.

Column grouping with named headers and collapsibility would be a major improvement in usability for teams managing complex boards.

posts.author: asplundsoftware (Brad)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @zs2technologies

Can you take a look at Column Groups - monday.com Marketplace ? I think it may do nearly what you are hoping for. The one thing that’s missing is keeping the header name visible when the column or group is collapsed.

How were you envisioning the name showing up for a collapsed column? Would it be like angled upwards, or visible on mouseover or something?

Currently, you can use it to color groups of columns (including groups of one, meaning you can effectively color individual columns), which does provide a quick visual of what the column is for once you use it for a bit, but I’m curious how a name might show up in your ideal world?

Thanks for insights!

Cheers,
Brad
